verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is secreted as a latent complex: the propeptide (LAP) remains non-covalently bound to mature TGFB1, and the latent complex is covalently tethered to cell-surface/ECM via LTBPs and to GARP/LRRC33 on cells (notably Tregs, platelets) through disulfide bonds. GARP-anchored latent TGFB1 is a wash-resistant cell-surface pool and a validated antibody target (e.g., anti-GARP/TGFB1 complex programs). This is stable surface attachment to a TM partner, not free secretion.
